sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Hilton shares fall 2% on weak full-year guidance despite Q1 beat
feed.summary: Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (NYSE:HLT) saw its shares fall over 2% in pre-market trading despite reporting Q1 earnings that beat analyst expectations. The dip was primarily due to the company's full-year adjusted EPS guidance of $8.79 to $8.91, which fell below the consensus estimate of $9.05. Although revenue missed slightly, RevPAR increased, and the company plans to return $3.5 billion to shareholders for the full year.

feed.title: Blackstone Inc. stock (US09259E1082): Is private credit growth strong enough to unlock sustained upside?
feed.summary: Blackstone Inc. is leveraging its private credit business as a primary growth driver, appealing to investors seeking higher yields in a low-rate environment. The firm's scale and diversified alternative asset management model, including permanent capital vehicles, provide stability and potential for long-term returns. While competitive advantages are strong, investors should be mindful of execution risks, interest rate sensitivities, and regulatory scrutiny on private markets.

feed.title: Joint Corp. sells 45 California clinics for $2.3 million
feed.summary: The Joint Corp. (NASDAQ:JYNT) has sold 45 corporate-managed chiropractic clinics in Southern California to Elite Chiro Group for approximately $2.3 million, as part of a strategic shift to a capital-light, pure-play franchisor model. This transaction significantly reduces the company's corporate-managed clinics and aligns with "The Joint 2.0" growth strategy. Analysts predict profitability this year and an EPS forecast of $0.29 for fiscal 2026, supported by the company's strong gross profit margin.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Price Target on NRG Energy to $198 From $197, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has adjusted its price target for NRG Energy (NYSE:NRG) to $198, a slight increase from its previous target of $197. The firm maintained its "Buy" rating on the stock. This update reflects Goldman Sachs' continued positive outlook on the energy company.

feed.title: State of Michigan Retirement System Sells 12,300 Shares of American Tower Corporation $AMT
feed.summary: The State of Michigan Retirement System reduced its stake in American Tower Corporation (AMT) by 9.1%, selling 12,300 shares but still retaining over 122,000 shares valued at approximately $21.52 million. This comes after American Tower reported an EPS miss for the quarter ($1.75 vs. $2.54 expected) despite a 7.5% revenue increase to $2.74 billion and favorable FY2026 guidance. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to $1.79 per share, while insider activity showed mixed signals with a COO selling shares and a director purchasing them.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet (NYSE: ZBH) lifts 2026 EPS outlook after strong Q1 results
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. reported strong Q1 2026 results with net sales up 9.3% to $2.087 billion and adjusted diluted EPS increasing 15.5% to $2.09. Following this performance, the company raised its full-year 2026 adjusted diluted EPS guidance to a range of $8.40 to $8.55. The report also detailed solid cash generation, significant share repurchases, and ongoing innovation efforts, while noting a pending final assessment on goodwill impairment which could impact GAAP net earnings.

feed.title: Michigan power provider CMS lifts Q1 EPS to $1.10, keeps 2026 view
feed.summary: CMS Energy reported Q1 2026 diluted EPS of $1.10 and adjusted EPS of $1.13, exceeding previous year's figures. The company reaffirmed its 2026 adjusted EPS guidance of $3.83–$3.90, expressing confidence towards the high end of this range, and also maintained its long-term adjusted EPS growth target of 6–8%. A webcast to discuss these results is scheduled for April 28, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. EDT.

feed.title: State of Michigan Retirement System Reduces Stock Position in Cummins Inc. $CMI
feed.summary: The State of Michigan Retirement System reduced its holdings in Cummins Inc. by 11.1% in the fourth quarter, selling 4,506 shares and now owning 36,131 shares valued at $18.44 million. Company insiders also sold a significant number of shares totaling approximately $18.4 million, reducing their ownership to 0.30%. Despite the sales, Cummins exceeded quarterly earnings and revenue expectations, and its stock is trading near a one-year high.

feed.title: Late-Breaking AFib Data on Pulsed Field Ablation Could Be A Game Changer For Abbott (ABT)
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories reported promising late-breaking data from trials in its pulsed field ablation and conduction system pacing portfolios, showcasing strong safety and clinical outcomes for complex atrial fibrillation. These results, particularly the successful treatment of complex AFib using pulsed field ablation alone, reinforce Abbott's position in next-generation cardiac rhythm management technologies. While this strengthens its device pipeline, the company's broader investment narrative still hinges on balancing innovation with pressures in diagnostics and diabetes care pricing.

feed.title: Halliburton Company Just Beat EPS By 10%: Here's What Analysts Think Will Happen Next
feed.summary: Halliburton Company reported strong first-quarter results, beating EPS estimates by 10% with earnings of US$0.55 per share. Analysts have responded positively, upgrading their revenue and earnings forecasts for 2026 and increasing their price target for the stock to US$41.64. Despite a projected revenue decline for Halliburton, the overall sentiment remains optimistic following the better-than-expected performance.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Adjusts Price Target on SLB to $63 From $60, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has raised its price target for SLB (formerly Schlumberger N.V.) to $63 from $60, while reaffirming a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook from the investment bank for the oilfield services company. The article also notes other analysts' recent price target adjustments for SLB.

feed.title: State of Michigan Retirement System Decreases Stock Holdings in Johnson Controls International plc $JCI
feed.summary: The State of Michigan Retirement System reduced its holdings in Johnson Controls International plc (NYSE:JCI) by 9.6% in the fourth quarter, selling 17,000 shares but still owning 159,956 shares valued at $19.16 million. Despite this decrease, institutional investors collectively own 90.05% of the company, with various other hedge funds significantly increasing their positions. Johnson Controls recently exceeded earnings expectations with $0.89 EPS and $5.80 billion in revenue, offering a quarterly dividend of $0.40 and maintaining a "Hold" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: State of Michigan Retirement System Grows Holdings in Copart, Inc. $CPRT
feed.summary: The State of Michigan Retirement System increased its stake in Copart, Inc. (NASDAQ:CPRT) by 13.4% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 52,000 shares to hold a total of 439,408 shares valued at $17.2 million. Despite this increase in holdings by a major institutional investor, analysts currently have a "Hold" rating on Copart, with an average target price of $44.40. The company recently missed EPS and revenue estimates, and its CEO sold a significant number of shares.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips stock (US20825C1045): Is its disciplined capital strategy strong enough to unlock new upside?
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (US20825C1045) is an independent oil and gas exploration and production company focusing on high-return projects and shareholder returns. The company's strategy, which includes low-cost production from premium assets and a commitment to return at least 30% of cash flow to shareholders, helps it navigate volatile energy markets. This approach makes the stock appealing for investors seeking exposure to global energy demand and consistent income.

feed.title: State of Michigan Retirement System Has $22.09 Million Position in MSCI Inc $MSCI
feed.summary: The State of Michigan Retirement System increased its stake in MSCI Inc. by 10.6% to 38,502 shares, now valued at approximately $22.09 million. This move comes amidst mixed insider activity, with CEO Henry A. Fernandez buying shares and CFO Andrew C. Wiechmann selling some, and positive analyst ratings with a consensus "Buy" and an average price target of $692.70. MSCI also reported a quarterly earnings beat and declared a quarterly dividend of $2.05 per share.

feed.title: Consumer demand lifts Kimberly-Clark to $4.2B, leaves 2026 view intact
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark reported Q1 2026 net sales of $4.2 billion, a 2.7% increase, with organic sales growth of 2.5%, largely driven by resilient consumer demand and innovation. The company's adjusted operating profit rose 3.7% to $732 million, while adjusted EPS from continuing operations decreased by 1.2% to $1.60 due to a higher effective tax rate. Kimberly-Clark reaffirmed its 2026 outlook, projecting mid-to-high single-digit adjusted operating profit growth and double-digit adjusted EPS growth on a constant-currency basis.

feed.title: Immunic Appoints Accomplished Biopharmaceutical Executive Michael A. Panzara, M.D., M.P.H., as Chief Medical Officer
feed.summary: Immunic, Inc., a late-stage biotechnology company, has appointed Michael A. Panzara, M.D., M.P.H., as its new Chief Medical Officer, effective April 24, 2026. Dr. Panzara brings over 25 years of neurology experience and will lead the company's development organization, including clinical and regulatory affairs, with a focus on advancing vidofludimus calcium through pivotal trials for multiple sclerosis. This appointment marks a critical step for Immunic as it transitions towards becoming a fully integrated commercial-stage company.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet finance chief departs; controller becomes interim CFO
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet (NYSE: ZBH) announced that its Chief Financial Officer, Suketu Upadhyay, will depart on April 28, 2026, for a new professional opportunity. Paul Stellato, the current Controller and Chief Accounting Officer, has been appointed interim CFO while the company searches for a permanent successor. Stellato brings over 20 years of finance and investor relations experience to the role, having joined Zimmer Biomet in May 2022.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet Announces Chief Financial Officer Transition
feed.summary: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. announced that Suketu Upadhyay, Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President of Finance, Operations and Supply Chain, will depart the company. Paul Stellato, currently Controller and Chief Accounting Officer, has been named interim CFO while a search for a permanent successor is conducted. Ivan Tornos, Chairman, President and CEO, expressed gratitude for Upadhyay's contributions and confidence in Stellato's leadership during this transition.

feed.title: AI tools are widening API attack risks, Akamai survey finds
feed.summary: A new survey by Akamai (NASDAQ: AKAM) reveals that 87% of organizations experienced an API-related security incident in the past year, with an average cost exceeding US$700,000 per incident. The study highlights significant gaps in API visibility and a growing risk from AI-related targeting, as 42% of security professionals reported attacks on APIs powering AI applications and LLMs. The findings underscore that without adequate security and testing, the rapid deployment of APIs, especially those integrated with AI, leaves organizations vulnerable.

feed.title: Security Leaders Cite AI as a Risk Multiplier for APIs in New Akamai Survey
feed.summary: A new Akamai survey reveals that 87% of organizations experienced an API-related security incident in the past year, with an average cost exceeding US$700,000 per incident. The study highlights that while organizations rush to deploy APIs, particularly for AI applications, they often lack adequate security and testing, leading to increased vulnerability. Security professionals are also concerned about API visibility, which has worsened with AI adoption, and rank securing AI technologies as their top cybersecurity priority for the coming year.

feed.title: SLB Q1 2026 Earnings: Revenue Growth Amid Middle East Disruptions
feed.summary: SLB reported its Q1 2026 financial results, with total revenue increasing by 3% to $8.72 billion but net income falling by 6% to $752 million, primarily due to disruptions in the Middle East. While North American revenue grew significantly, international revenue saw a decline. The company is actively pursuing strategic acquisitions to enhance its production systems and technology offerings.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Iridium Communications (IRDM) After Its Recent Share Price Surge?
feed.summary: Iridium Communications (IRDM) stock has seen significant price swings, including a 110.6% gain year-to-date. A Discounted Cash Flow analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 59.4% at $37.40, while a Price-to-Earnings ratio of 37.46x indicates it might be overvalued compared to industry averages and its Fair Ratio. The article presents bull and bear case narratives, with fair value estimates ranging from $30.38 to $38.60 per share, highlighting the debate around its future growth and valuation.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (ALLE) Allegion plc Reports Q1 Revenue $1.03B, vs. FactSet Est of $1.03B
feed.summary: Allegion plc (ALLE) reported Q1 revenue of $1.03 billion, matching FactSet's estimate. The company projects full-year 2026 adjusted EPS to be between $8.70 and $8.90, compared to FactSet's estimate of $8.80. Additionally, Allegion plc announced a quarterly dividend of $0.55 per share, payable on June 30, 2026.

feed.title: Cisco Systems Inc. stock (US17275R1023): Is networking demand shifting enough to drive sustained gro
feed.summary: Cisco Systems Inc. is adapting to the evolving tech landscape, driven by AI and cloud infrastructure, while maintaining its core networking powerhouse status. The company's shift towards software subscriptions and strategic acquisitions like Splunk aim to boost margins and solidify its market position. Investors are evaluating whether these changes can lead to sustained growth and increased shareholder value amid market volatility and competitive challenges.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) issues callable zero‑coupon notes, 6.10% yield to 2056
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has priced callable zero-coupon notes with an original issue price of $169.254 per $1,000 principal, yielding 6.10% to maturity on May 6, 2056. These notes do not pay periodic interest but accrete in value, and the issuer retains the option to redeem them annually from May 6, 2028, through May 6, 2055, at scheduled accreted principal amounts. Investors face risks including credit risk of JPMorgan Chase, potential early redemption, and the notes' long maturity relative to other fixed-income products.

feed.title: Top 2 Utilities Stocks That Could Lead To Your Biggest Gains This Quarter
feed.summary: This article highlights two oversold utility stocks, PG&E Corp (PCG) and VivoPower PLC (VIVO), which may offer significant gains. Both companies are trading with Relative Strength Index (RSI) values below 30, indicating they are oversold, and have recently reported developments that could contribute to their recovery. PG&E posted strong quarterly earnings and VivoPower announced a new high-margin revenue stream.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark leads all large-cap consumer staples stocks in YoY CapEx growth (XLP:NYSEARCA)
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark is highlighted as the top large-cap consumer staples stock for year-over-year capital expenditure (CapEx) growth. The article indicates that increasing CapEx often suggests a company's strong belief in its future growth. It also notes that Altria Group and Target are among other leaders in CapEx growth within this sector.

feed.title: P&G faces $1B annual hit from Iran war oil prices
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble Co. is expected to face a $1 billion annual increase in costs due to rising oil prices, largely attributed to the Iran war. This financial hit comes as the company posted its fiscal third-quarter results. The soaring oil prices are impacting not only taxpayers but also major corporations like P&G.

feed.title: JD.com Sets May 12 Board Meeting and Investor Call for First-Quarter 2026 Results
feed.summary: JD.com has scheduled a board meeting for May 12, 2026, to approve and release its unaudited first-quarter 2026 financial results. The company will also host a conference call and webcast to discuss the results, ensuring coordinated disclosure for its Hong Kong and U.S. shareholders. This approach highlights JD.com's commitment to transparent communication with its global investor base regarding its performance in the competitive e-commerce landscape.

feed.title: BMO reiterates Bloom Energy stock rating on Oracle data center deal
feed.summary: BMO Capital has reiterated a Market Perform rating and an $188.00 price target for Bloom Energy Corp. following an update on its agreement with Oracle Corp. Oracle's "Project Jupiter" outlines a clearer deployment roadmap for Bloom Energy's hardware, with a potential capacity of 2.45 gigawatts, and suggests a full pivot to Bloom fuel cells over conventional generation methods. Despite the stock surging over 1,100% in the past year, InvestingPro data indicates it is currently overvalued, though analysts expect Bloom Energy to become profitable this year.

feed.title: Centene Reports Strong Q1 Earnings, Raises Guidance
feed.summary: Centene reported strong Q1 earnings, with non-GAAP EPS of $3.37 surpassing expectations by $1.24 and revenue reaching $49.94 billion, a 7.1% year-over-year increase. The company also raised its 2026 premium and service revenue guidance by $1.0 billion to a range of $171.0 billion to $175.0 billion, and updated its 2026 GAAP diluted EPS guidance floor to greater than $2.37 and adjusted diluted EPS guidance floor to greater than $3.40. Analysts have reacted by modest price target increases, with Jefferies raising their target to $39 and BofA to $34, though both maintained their current ratings.

feed.title: CDW Corp. stock (US1258961002): Is its IT solutions edge strong enough to unlock new upside?
feed.summary: CDW Corp. is positioned as a leading IT distribution and services provider, leveraging its consultative selling model and partnerships with major vendors to offer tailored technology solutions. The company's diversified customer base, focus on high-margin services, and strong presence in English-speaking markets are highlighted as key drivers for sustained growth. While competitive pressures and economic uncertainties pose risks, analysts view CDW favorably due to its robust services growth and ability to navigate fluctuating tech spend environments.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets stock (US12514G1085): Is its exchange dominance strong enough to unlock new upsi
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets, identified by ISIN US12514G1085, is presented as a strong investment opportunity due to its foundational role in U.S. and global exchange infrastructure, particularly in options and futures trading. The company's core business model, centered on transaction fees, market data, and a focus on organic growth and technology, creates a robust competitive advantage. Analysts generally view Cboe favorably, highlighting its durable market moat and potential upside from increasing trading volumes and product innovation.

feed.title: UBS Group AG Purchases 30,008 Shares of L3Harris Technologies Inc $LHX
feed.summary: UBS Group AG increased its holdings in L3Harris Technologies Inc. by 2.9% in Q4, acquiring 30,008 additional shares to bring its total to 1,076,714 shares valued at $316.1 million. L3Harris reported Q4 EPS of $2.86, beating estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $1.25. The company currently holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $349.55, and institutional investors own approximately 84.8% of its stock.

feed.title: Sanctuary Advisors LLC Grows Stake in L3Harris Technologies Inc $LHX
feed.summary: Sanctuary Advisors LLC increased its stake in L3Harris Technologies (NYSE:LHX) by 11.4% in Q4, acquiring 3,788 additional shares to own a total of 36,943 shares worth $10.85 million. Despite this, company insiders sold 19,025 shares totaling $6.82 million over the last quarter, reducing their ownership to about 0.68%. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for LHX with an average target price of $349.55, and the company offers an annual dividend of $5.00, yielding approximately 1.6%.

feed.title: UBS Group AG Sells 1,515,585 Shares of Welltower Inc. $WELL
feed.summary: UBS Group AG significantly reduced its holdings in Welltower Inc. by selling over 1.5 million shares in Q4, though it still retains a substantial stake. Welltower reported strong Q4 earnings, exceeding revenue and EPS estimates, and provided a positive outlook for FY2026. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for the real estate investment trust, with a consensus price target around $225.43.

feed.title: Broadwood and Neal Bradsher hold 39.7% of Insight Molecular (IMDX) shares
feed.summary: Broadwood Partners, Broadwood Capital, and Neal C. Bradsher have reported a combined beneficial ownership of 12,835,134 shares, or 39.7%, of Insight Molecular Diagnostics (IMDX). This stake is based on 32,318,071 outstanding shares, adjusted to include warrants they beneficially own. The filing indicates the group intends to continuously review their investment and may engage with company management and the board to explore strategic alternatives for enhancing shareholder value.

feed.title: Sanctuary Advisors LLC Lowers Holdings in Apollo Global Management Inc. $APO
feed.summary: Sanctuary Advisors LLC reduced its stake in Apollo Global Management Inc. by 14.2% in Q4, selling 13,305 shares and retaining 80,322 shares valued at $11.63 million. This reduction comes amid heightened legal risks for Apollo related to alleged payments and ties to Leon Black and Jeffrey Epstein, which have led to multiple law firms filing or soliciting lead-plaintiff roles in securities class actions. Despite these challenges, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Apollo Global Management with a consensus target price of $149.42, although some brokers have recently trimmed their price targets.

feed.title: IES Holdings Announces Fiscal 2026 Second Quarter Results Earnings Release Schedule
feed.summary: IES Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: IESC) stated that it will disclose its fiscal 2026 second quarter earnings before the market opens on Friday, May 1, 2026. The company specializes in designing and installing integrated electrical and technology systems, serving various markets including data centers and residential housing. IES employs over 11,000 individuals across the United States.

feed.title: UPS (NYSE: UPS) Q1 2026 earnings drop but full-year outlook held
feed.summary: UPS reported Q1 2026 revenues of $21.2 billion and diluted EPS of $1.02, missing prior-year figures. Despite the earnings drop, the company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance, targeting approximately $89.7 billion in revenue and a 9.6% non-GAAP adjusted operating margin, citing ongoing transformation efforts and cost savings.

feed.title: Tesla moves to grant Musk $114B in stock under 2018 pay plan
feed.summary: Tesla is moving to grant CEO Elon Musk 303,960,630 shares of common stock, valued at over $114 billion, under his 2018 pay package. This decision follows the Delaware Supreme Court's restoration of the package after it was previously invalidated, and Tesla has since reincorporated in Texas. Musk will need to meet significant operational targets, such as deploying one million robotaxis or delivering 20 million vehicles, and fulfill continuous service through January 2028 to receive the full payout.

feed.title: Campbell Soup Co. stock (US1280301048): Is its snack moat strong enough to unlock new upside?
feed.summary: Campbell Soup Co. has strategically shifted its focus from traditional soups to higher-margin snacks like Goldfish crackers and Pepperidge Farm cookies, leveraging strong brand loyalty to maintain a competitive moat. Despite challenges from private labels and consumer health trends, analysts view the stock as a defensive play with potential for long-term gains if innovation and execution in international markets accelerate. The company's ability to balance indulgence with health-conscious offerings and navigate commodity volatility will be key to unlocking further upside.

feed.title: Centene: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Centene Corp. reported a strong first quarter, with profit reaching $1.54 billion, or $3.11 per share. Adjusted earnings were $3.37 per share, surpassing Wall Street expectations. The healthcare company also exceeded revenue forecasts, posting $37.3 billion, and projects full-year revenue between $175.5 billion and $179.5 billion.

feed.title: Centene: Q1 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Centene Corp. reported a strong first quarter with profits of $1.54 billion, or $3.11 per share. Adjusted earnings per share reached $3.37, significantly exceeding analyst expectations of $1.87. The healthcare company also surpassed revenue forecasts, posting $49.94 billion against an expected $47.47 billion, and projects full-year revenue between $187.5 billion and $191.5 billion.

feed.title: BofA raises Cincinnati Financial stock price target on resilient results
feed.summary: BofA Securities has increased its price target for Cincinnati Financial (NASDAQ:CINF) to $183 from $177, maintaining a Buy rating. This adjustment follows the insurance company's resilient financial results, notably its Q4 2025 earnings per share of $3.37, which surpassed expectations despite higher-than-estimated catastrophe losses. The company is highlighted for its strong financial foundation, consistent dividend payments for 54 consecutive years, and undervalued stock price according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: Odfjell Technology awarded long term contract with ConocoPhillips
feed.summary: Odfjell Technology has secured a 5.5-year contract with ConocoPhillips Skandinavia AS for integrated services in the North Sea, extending until December 31, 2031, with options for up to an additional ten years. This agreement covers platform drilling operations, maintenance, tubular running, well engineering, and planning in Norway, significantly boosting Odfjell Technology's firm backlog and reinforcing its market growth ambitions. The contract also includes options for downhole equipment and Permanent Plug & Abandonment execution.

feed.title: [6-K] ING GROEP NV Current Report (Foreign Issuer) | ING SEC Filing - Form 6-K
feed.summary: ING Groep N.V. filed a 6-K Current Report detailing the progress of its €1.1 billion share buyback program. The company repurchased 1,358,806 shares between April 20-24, 2026, at an average price of €24.06, totaling €32,694,625.98 for the week. Since the program began on October 30, 2025, ING has repurchased 46,660,057 shares, representing approximately 99.49% of the maximum planned value.

feed.title: Fortinet (NASDAQ:FTNT) Stock Rating Upgraded by Arete Research
feed.summary: Arete Research has upgraded Fortinet (NASDAQ:FTNT) from a "strong sell" to a "strong-buy" rating. This upgrade comes as Fortinet's stock trades up, with the company reporting strong quarterly earnings that beat analyst estimates. Insider selling by the CEO and VP has also been noted, alongside significant institutional investor activity.

feed.title: Cohu (NASDAQ:COHU) Raised to Strong-Buy at Jefferies Financial Group
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group upgraded Cohu (NASDAQ:COHU) to a "strong-buy" rating. This upgrade comes as Cohu reported quarterly EPS of ($0.15), missing estimates, despite revenue of $122.2 million which was up 29.9% year-over-year. The company has an average rating of "Moderate Buy" from analysts, with a consensus price target of $37.50.
